title: dichanthelium commutatum subsp. ashei
intro: Dichanthelium commutatum subsp. ashei, also known as Ashe's rosette grass or Ashe's rosette grass Dichanthelium, is a subspecies of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that grows in dry to moist woods and is native to the southeastern United States. Traditionally, Dichanthelium commutatum subsp. ashei has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and its health benefits include promoting wound healing, reducing fever, and supporting immune function.
